reasoning: French Polynesia is highly stable politically and lacks any organized armed groups. ACLED explicitly defines "Battles" as violent interactions between two organized armed groups. With strict gun control, an absence of active insurgency, and no history of modern armed conflict (the last being in the 19th century), the likelihood of experiencing more than 10 "Battles" in any 30-day window is virtually nonexistent. Even the recent severe unrest in New Caledonia—which hasn't spilled over to French Polynesia—is predominantly characterized as protests and riots rather than battles. The probabilities are set at the minimum allowable threshold of 0.05 for the nearest date, increasing marginally for the further dates to account for the inherent, albeit minimal, uncertainty over longer time horizons.
